tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: The user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: Let me compile a good list of hotel recommendations across different budgets and neighborhoods, then save them as a memory.

Here are some great LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets. Let me save these for you!

---

### 🏨 **Luxury Picks**

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
Tucked within a secluded canyon, Hotel Bel-Air is one of Los Angeles' most iconic hideaways, a sanctuary for Hollywood legends for over eight decades.
It really does feel tucked away from it all — 12 acres of private bliss up in the canyons above Los Angeles.

Rooms from ~$1,183/night.

2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
The Beverly Hills Hotel has a red carpet running up its entrance steps — this Dorchester Collection icon is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms start at $1,145 per night.
3. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —

Comes with a sterling pedigree (the group also operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London), exuding refined elegance with 205 redesigned guest rooms featuring mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
4. **Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA** —
Commands some of the best panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking glass tower, and within strolling distance of the L.A. Live complex including the Crypto.com Arena.

### 🌊 **Beach/Coastal Picks**

5. **Casa del Mar (Santa Monica)** —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
6. **The Georgian (Santa Monica)** —
Striking Art Deco architecture meets modern sophistication at this historic 1930s coastal landmark, captivating visitors with curated art exhibitions and vibrant live music.

### 💎 **Best Value / Mid-Range**

7. **The London West Hollywood** —

Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood. All 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft (the largest in the city), and every room includes a private balcony, wet bar, Italian linens, and rooftop pool with panoramic views.
8. **Hotel Figueroa (Downtown LA)** —

One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with beautifully appointed rooms and standout shared spaces.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making this one of the better values in central LA.
9. **The LINE L.A. (Koreatown)** —
Features a rooftop pool, in-room mini-bar stocked with Korean treats, and an on-site nightclub.

Starting around $179/night.

### 🆕 **New for 2026**

10. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
One of six new hotels in early 2026, Ian Schrager's PUBLIC West Hollywood sits in the former Standard building.
11. **The Anthem Hotel** —
This 179-room property opened in February 2026 as the first lifestyle hotel in LA's Stadium District, near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater.

---

**💡 Pro Tip:**

The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose — Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings, Santa Monica delivers beach access and walkability, Downtown LA has options near cultural attractions, and West Hollywood has restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.
Let me save these recommendations now!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - June 2026 Trip
params.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip (week of June 2026):

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air — Secluded canyon retreat, 12 acres, iconic. ~$1,183/night
2. The Beverly Hills Hotel — Quintessential LA icon, Dorchester Collection. ~$1,145/night
3. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — Refined elegance, mid-century design, Whisky Bar, rooftop Dante restaurant
4. Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA — Best panoramic city views, top 4 floors of glass tower, near Crypto.com Arena

BEACH/COASTAL:
5. Casa del Mar (Santa Monica) — Direct beach access, $692–$1,606/night
6. The Georgian (Santa Monica) — Art Deco, historic 1930s landmark, live music

BEST VALUE / MID-RANGE:
7. The London West Hollywood — Best luxury-to-value in WeHo, largest rooms (750+ sq ft), balconies, rooftop pool
8. Hotel Figueroa (Downtown LA) — Spanish Colonial, great pool & dining, $200–$400/night
9. The LINE L.A. (Koreatown) — Rooftop pool, Korean treats mini-bar, ~$179/night

NEW FOR 2026:
10. PUBLIC West Hollywood — Ian Schrager, former Standard building
11. The Anthem Hotel — Stadium District, near SoFi Stadium, opened Feb 2026

TIP: Match neighborhood to purpose — Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach, DTLA for culture, WeHo for nightlife/dining.

result.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip (week of June 2026):

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air — Secluded canyon retreat, 12 acres, iconic. ~$1,183/night
2. The Beverly Hills Hotel — Quintessential LA icon, Dorchester Collection. ~$1,145/night
3. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — Refined elegance, mid-century design, Whisky Bar, rooftop Dante restaurant
4. Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA — Best panoramic city views, top 4 floors of glass tower, near Crypto.com Arena

BEACH/COASTAL:
5. Casa del Mar (Santa Monica) — Direct beach access, $692–$1,606/night
6. The Georgian (Santa Monica) — Art Deco, historic 1930s landmark, live music

BEST VALUE / MID-RANGE:
7. The London West Hollywood — Best luxury-to-value in WeHo, largest rooms (750+ sq ft), balconies, rooftop pool
8. Hotel Figueroa (Downtown LA) — Spanish Colonial, great pool & dining, $200–$400/night
9. The LINE L.A. (Koreatown) — Rooftop pool, Korean treats mini-bar, ~$179/night

NEW FOR 2026:
10. PUBLIC West Hollywood — Ian Schrager, former Standard building
11. The Anthem Hotel — Stadium District, near SoFi Stadium, opened Feb 2026

TIP: Match neighborhood to purpose — Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach, DTLA for culture, WeHo for nightlife/dining.
result.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - June 2026 Trip
